winner.title: Machine-readable conformance test vectors (JSON)
winner.description: Produce 50+ JSON test vectors organized by circuit (HumanUniqueness, AgentPolicy, Delegation) and scenario: valid witness, expired credential (expiryTimestamp < currentTimestamp), revoked human nullifier, stale Merkle root not in history buffer, scope subset violation (delegateeScope & ~delegatorScope != 0), cumulative bit encoding violation (bit 4 without bits 2+3), delegation chain at depths 1/2/3/4 (4 must fail), and replay with reused nonce. Each vector specifies inputs, expected outputs, and whether the proof MUST succeed or MUST fail with a specific error code. This is the minimum bar for any second implementation to claim conformance.

outline.title: Machine-readable conformance test vectors (JSON)

artifacts.description: JSON Schema defining the conformance vector envelope: id, circuit, scenario, inputs, expected_output, must_succeed, error_code
Test vectors for HumanUniqueness circuit: valid witness, expired credential, revoked nullifier, stale Merkle root, replay with reused nonce
Test vectors for AgentPolicy circuit: valid witness, scope subset violation (delegateeScope & ~delegatorScope != 0), cumulative bit encoding violation (bit 4 without bits 2+3)
Test vectors for Delegation circuit: chain depths 1/2/3 (must succeed), depth 4 (must fail), scope inheritance violations, expired delegation credential
Canonical error code registry referenced by vectors: ERR_EXPIRED_CREDENTIAL, ERR_REVOKED_NULLIFIER, ERR_STALE_ROOT, ERR_SCOPE_VIOLATION, ERR_BIT_ENCODING, ERR_CHAIN_DEPTH, ERR_REPLAY_NONCE
CI harness: loads all vector files, validates against schema, runs each vector through the reference prover/verifier, asserts must_succeed/error_code outcomes
Conformance guide: how to run vectors against a new implementation, pass criteria, how to add new vectors, versioning policy

steps: Define JSON Schema for vector envelope (id, circuit, scenario, inputs, expected_output.proof_valid, expected_output.error_code, notes)
Enumerate error code registry (ERR_EXPIRED_CREDENTIAL, ERR_REVOKED_NULLIFIER, ERR_STALE_ROOT, ERR_SCOPE_VIOLATION, ERR_BIT_ENCODING, ERR_CHAIN_DEPTH, ERR_REPLAY_NONCE) and publish as ERROR_CODES.md

Author HumanUniqueness vectors: 1 valid witness (must succeed), 1 expired credential (expiryTimestamp < currentTimestamp, must fail ERR_EXPIRED_CREDENTIAL), 1 revoked nullifier (must fail ERR_REVOKED_NULLIFIER), 1 stale Merkle root not in history buffer (must fail ERR_STALE_ROOT), 2 replay vectors with reused nonce (must fail ERR_REPLAY_NONCE)

Author AgentPolicy vectors: 1 valid witness, 3 scope subset violations with differing bit masks (delegateeScope & ~delegatorScope != 0, must fail ERR_SCOPE_VIOLATION), 2 cumulative bit encoding violations (bit 4 set without bits 2+3, must fail ERR_BIT_ENCODING), 1 valid edge-case with all bits set

Author Delegation chain vectors: depth 1 valid (succeed), depth 2 valid (succeed), depth 3 valid (succeed), depth 4 (must fail ERR_CHAIN_DEPTH), depth 2 with scope escalation (must fail ERR_SCOPE_VIOLATION), depth 3 with expired intermediate credential (must fail ERR_EXPIRED_CREDENTIAL)
Pad each circuit file to reach 50+ total vectors: add boundary-value inputs (timestamp == expiryTimestamp, root at history buffer boundary, scope == 0, scope == all-bits-set)

Write validate_vectors.py: parse all JSON files, validate schema, invoke reference prover for must_succeed cases and assert valid proof, invoke reference verifier for must_fail cases and assert correct error_code
Integrate validate_vectors.py into CI (GitHub Actions) as a blocking check on every PR touching circuits or vectors
Write conformance/README.md: pass criteria definition, how to point the harness at an alternative implementation, versioning (semver on vector set), contribution guide for new scenarios
dependencies: Reference circuit implementations for HumanUniqueness, AgentPolicy, Delegation (Circom or equivalent)
Reference prover/verifier CLI or library callable from validate_vectors.py
Canonical definition of history buffer size (needed for stale-root boundary vectors)
Canonical max delegation chain depth (confirmed as 3)
Canonical scope bit layout (bit positions 0-N, cumulative encoding rules)

winner.title: Add delegatee Merkle inclusion proof to Delegation circuit
winner.description: The Delegation circuit accepts delegateeCredCommitment as a private input but never proves it exists in any Merkle tree. An attacker can fabricate a credential commitment, receive a valid delegation, and use the resulting scopeCommitment downstream. Add a Merkle inclusion proof (reusing the BinaryMerkleRoot template at MAX_DEPTH=20) against the agentTree root, passed as a public input and checked on-chain against the root history buffer. This is the single highest-severity soundness bug in the current circuit set.

outline.title: Add delegatee Merkle inclusion proof to Delegation circuit

artifacts.description: Add agentTreeRoot as public input; add merkleProof (pathElements[20], pathIndices[20]) as private inputs; instantiate BinaryMerkleRoot(20) component; constrain root output === agentTreeRoot; wire delegateeCredCommitment as leaf
Existing BinaryMerkleRoot(MAX_DEPTH) template — verify it is already present and importable; no changes expected
Regenerated Groth16 verifier from updated circuit; replace existing verifier bytecode
Accept agentTreeRoot as calldata param; validate it exists in the on-chain root history buffer (shared with IdentityRegistry); revert if root is unknown or expired
Updated circuit specification: public/private input table, Merkle proof format, root history buffer interface, soundness argument for delegatee inclusion
Circom unit tests: valid proof passes; proof with fabricated commitment (leaf not in tree) fails constraint; proof with wrong root fails; proof with correct leaf at varying depths passes
Solidity tests: submit delegation with valid root in history → success; submit with stale/unknown root → revert; replay attack with old root → revert

Update proof generation helper to accept agentTree MerkleProof witness (pathElements, pathIndices, leaf index); fetch current agentTreeRoot from on-chain or cached state; pass all inputs to snarkjs fullProve
Document the phantom delegatee vulnerability, the fix, the trust model for agentTreeRoot, and upgrade path for existing delegations

steps: Audit BinaryMerkleRoot(MAX_DEPTH) template: confirm it accepts leaf + pathElements[MAX_DEPTH] + pathIndices[MAX_DEPTH] and outputs root; verify it is poseidon-hashed and matches the hash used for delegateeCredCommitment

Update circuits/delegation/delegation.circom: add signal input agentTreeRoot (public); add signal input merklePathElements[20] and merklePathIndices[20] (private); add component merkleCheck = BinaryMerkleRoot(20); wire leaf = delegateeCredCommitment, path inputs, and constrain merkleCheck.root === agentTreeRoot
Recompile circuit with circom 2.x; run snarkjs groth16 setup with new .r1cs; regenerate proving key and verification key; export new verifier contract
Replace DelegationVerifier.sol with regenerated verifier; update ABI references throughout contracts/

Update DelegationRegistry.sol: add agentTreeRoot parameter to submitDelegation(); call IRootHistory.isKnownRoot(agentTreeRoot) and revert with UnknownAgentRoot() if false; emit agentTreeRoot in DelegationSubmitted event
Implement or verify IRootHistory interface is already shared with IdentityRegistry's root history buffer; if not, extract to shared library contract
Write circuit unit tests covering: (a) honest witness at depth 1..20, (b) fabricated leaf not in tree → expect constraint failure, (c) correct leaf wrong root → expect constraint failure
Write DelegationRegistry contract tests covering root validation, stale root rejection, and event emission
Update SDK prover.ts to build Merkle witness from agentTree snapshot; expose helper to fetch latest agentTreeRoot from chain
Update spec and security docs; bump circuit version constant in all relevant files
Run full test suite; verify no regressions in downstream circuits consuming scopeCommitment
Coordinate trusted setup ceremony or use existing Powers of Tau if constraint count fits; document entropy source
dependencies: BinaryMerkleRoot circom template (circuits/lib/binary_merkle_root.circom)
IRootHistory on-chain root buffer shared with IdentityRegistry
Poseidon hash consistency between delegateeCredCommitment construction and Merkle leaf hashing
Powers of Tau ceremony supporting at least 2^16 constraints
snarkjs >= 0.7 for Groth16 proof generation
circom >= 2.1.6

winner.title: Write formal invariant properties for all three circuits
winner.description: Define and machine-check the following properties using circom-compat tooling or a custom R1CS analyzer: (1) No unconstrained signals — every private input flows into at least one constraint that gates an output. (2) Nullifier uniqueness — for fixed (scope, secret) or (credentialCommitment, nonce), exactly one nullifier value satisfies the constraints. (3) Scope monotonicity — in Delegation, prove that for any satisfying witness, delegateeScope & ~delegatorScope == 0 is enforced (no bit escalation). (4) Field overflow absence — all Num2Bits(64) decompositions fully constrain their input to [0, 2^64). Deliver as a test harness with property assertions that run in CI, using either ecne (R1CS constraint analyzer) or picus (underconstrained signal detector).

outline.title: Write formal invariant properties for all three circuits

artifacts.description: Shell script that compiles each circuit to R1CS and runs ecne constraint analyzer, failing CI if any unconstrained signals are detected
Shell script that invokes picus on each circuit's R1CS to detect underconstrained private signals, producing a structured JSON report
Snarkjs-based property test: for fixed (scope, secret) and (credentialCommitment, nonce) inputs, enumerate multiple witness candidates and assert exactly one nullifier value satisfies all constraints

Property test for Delegation circuit: generates random (delegatorScope, delegateeScope) pairs where delegateeScope has bits outside delegatorScope, asserts witness generation fails (constraint violation)
Property test: attempts to supply values >= 2^64 to all Num2Bits(64) inputs across all three circuits, asserts constraint system rejects each overflow witness

Programmatic R1CS walker that parses compiled R1CS JSON, builds a signal-to-constraint bipartite graph, and asserts every private signal appears in at least one constraint that transitively gates a public output
Human-readable specification of all four invariant classes, the formal statement of each property, the tooling used to check it, and expected CI pass/fail semantics
Top-level CI entry point that orchestrates all property tests, collects results, and emits a structured summary with pass/fail per invariant per circuit
Registry mapping each circuit name to its .circom source path, compiled R1CS path, and which invariant checks apply to it

steps: Identify the three circuits under test (MembershipCredential, NullifierDerivation, Delegation) and compile each to R1CS + WASM using circom 2.x; record constraint counts and signal maps
Install and pin ecne and picus to specific versions in package.json / a Python venv; document invocation flags needed for bn128 field
Write circuits_under_test.json registry with source paths, R1CS paths, and applicable invariant checklist per circuit

Implement unconstrained_signals.test.ts: parse the R1CS JSON wire-to-constraint map, build adjacency, run reachability from each private signal to any labeled public output wire, fail if any private signal is unreachable
Run ecne_runner.sh against all three circuits and capture baseline output; fix any pre-existing under-constraint warnings before writing pass assertions
Run picus_runner.sh against all three circuits; resolve any flagged signals in source circom before locking the baseline

Implement nullifier_uniqueness.test.ts: use snarkjs fullProve with fixed inputs across a 256-sample random secret space, collect all nullifier outputs, assert the set has cardinality 1 per fixed (scope, secret) tuple
Implement scope_monotonicity.test.ts: generate 1000 random pairs where (delegateeScope & ~delegatorScope) != 0, call snarkjs wtns.calculate, assert each throws a constraint-unsatisfied error
Implement field_overflow.test.ts: for each Num2Bits(64) input signal, supply 2^64, 2^64+1, and 2^128-1, assert witness calculation fails; also supply 2^64-1 and assert it succeeds
Implement ci_harness.ts to run all four property test files sequentially, aggregate results into a JSON summary, exit non-zero on any failure
Add a GitHub Actions job (or extend existing CI) that: compiles circuits, runs ecne_runner.sh, runs picus_runner.sh, runs ci_harness.ts, uploads the JSON summary as a CI artifact
Write INVARIANTS.md with formal statements (using R1CS / arithmetic circuit notation) for all four invariant classes, mapping each to the specific test file and tooling that enforces it
Validate end-to-end: intentionally introduce an unconstrained signal in a scratch branch, confirm CI fails on the correct check, then revert
dependencies: circom 2.x compiler with bn128 support
snarkjs >= 0.7
ecne (R1CS constraint analyzer)
picus (underconstrained signal detector)
node >= 20 / ts-node for TypeScript test harness
Compiled R1CS artifacts for MembershipCredential, NullifierDerivation, and Delegation circuits
Existing circuit source files with Num2Bits(64) decompositions and nullifier templates

winner.title: did:bolyra DID Method Specification with VC issuance
winner.description: Define a W3C DID method `did:bolyra:<base58(identityCommitment)>` with a DID Document structure that includes a `BolyraVerification2024` verification method type pointing to the on-chain IdentityRegistry. Specify the resolution algorithm: given a DID, derive the identity commitment, query the humanTree or agentTree for membership, and return the DID Document with service endpoints. Define a Verifiable Credential schema for handshake results (issuer = verifier service, subject = did:bolyra:*, credentialSubject includes scopeCommitment and sessionNonce). This bridges Bolyra into the existing SSI ecosystem and enables interop with any W3C DID-compatible wallet or verifier.

outline.title: did:bolyra DID Method Specification with VC issuance

artifacts.description: W3C DID Method specification for did:bolyra, including method syntax, DID Document structure, CRUD operations, security considerations, and privacy considerations per W3C DID Core 1.0
JSON-LD Verifiable Credential schema for handshake results, defining credentialSubject fields: scopeCommitment, sessionNonce, verifierDID, humanityProofRoot, agentCapabilityRoot

Cryptographic suite specification for BolyraVerification2024 verification method type, defining key material representation (BN254 elliptic curve points), proof purpose, and DID Document embedding rules
On-chain DID Document registry anchoring service endpoints and key rotations; wraps IdentityRegistry with DID-resolution-friendly view functions keyed by identityCommitment
DID resolver implementing the did:bolyra resolution algorithm: decode DID → derive identityCommitment → query humanTree/agentTree membership → construct and return DID Document JSON-LD
VC issuance module: takes a completed handshake result, signs a BolyraHandshakeCredential as the verifier service DID, and returns a signed JWT-VC or JSON-LD Verifiable Credential
DID Document builder: constructs conformant DID Documents including verificationMethod, authentication, assertionMethod, and service endpoint sections from on-chain state
Unit and integration tests for the DID resolver: valid resolution, deactivated identity, non-member identity, malformed DID, and chain query failure paths
Tests for VC issuance and verification: schema conformance, signature verification, credential expiry, revocation check, and round-trip JWT-VC parse
Interoperability tests against did-resolver universal resolver interface and vc-js / @digitalbazaar/vc libraries to validate W3C ecosystem compatibility
W3C DID Specification Registries submission template and instructions for registering did:bolyra, including method-specific parameters and contact information

steps: Define the did:bolyra method syntax: did:bolyra:<base58btc-encoded-identity-commitment> (32-byte BN254 field element encoded as base58btc, no version or network prefix in v1)

Specify DID Document JSON-LD structure: @context includes W3C DID Core + bolyra-specific context; id = the DID; verificationMethod array with one BolyraVerification2024 entry containing the BN254 public key; authentication and assertionMethod reference the verification method; service array includes IdentityRegistry contract address as a LinkedDomains or custom type endpoint

Define BolyraVerification2024 cryptographic suite: key type = BN254G1 (matches Semaphore/Poseidon stack); publicKeyMultibase encodes the identity commitment; proof purpose mapping to Semaphore group membership proofs

Specify resolution algorithm: (1) parse DID, extract base58-encoded string, base58btc-decode to 32-byte identity commitment; (2) call IdentityRegistry.isMember(commitment, humanTreeId) or agentTree equivalent to verify membership and retrieve group root; (3) fetch any key rotation or service endpoint deltas from BolyraDidRegistry; (4) assemble DID Document and return with metadata including deactivated flag if commitment is no longer in tree

Specify CRUD operations: Create = calling IdentityRegistry.registerIdentity() constitutes DID creation, no separate on-chain DID create tx needed; Update = key rotation via BolyraDidRegistry.setServiceEndpoint(); Delete/Deactivate = calling IdentityRegistry.revokeIdentity(); Read = resolution algorithm above

Design BolyraHandshakeCredential VC schema: type = ['VerifiableCredential', 'BolyraHandshakeCredential']; issuer = did:bolyra:<verifier-service-identity-commitment>; credentialSubject.id = did:bolyra:<prover-identity-commitment>; credentialSubject.scopeCommitment = hex string; credentialSubject.sessionNonce = hex string; credentialSubject.verifiedAt = ISO8601 timestamp; credentialSubject.humanityAsserted = bool; expirationDate = sessionNonce-derived TTL

Implement BolyraDidRegistry contract: mapping(uint256 => ServiceEndpoint[]) for identity-commitment-keyed endpoints; event-based key rotation log; view function resolveDocument(uint256 commitment) returns packed DID Document fields; integrate with existing IdentityRegistry for membership checks

Implement SDK resolver.ts: conform to did-resolver DIDResolver interface (resolve(did, options) => DIDResolutionResult); handle did:bolyra method prefix guard; implement base58btc decode; call on-chain view functions via ethers.js provider; construct JSON-LD DID Document; set didDocumentMetadata.deactivated correctly

Implement SDK issuer.ts: accept HandshakeResult struct from existing protocol; build VC payload per schema; sign using verifier service's private key via did-jwt or jsonld-signatures; support both JWT-VC compact serialization and JSON-LD Linked Data Proofs output formats
Register bolyra-specific JSON-LD context at a stable URL (or inline in spec): define BolyraVerification2024, BolyraHandshakeCredential, scopeCommitment, sessionNonce, humanityAsserted terms

Write resolver tests covering: happy-path human identity, happy-path agent identity, deactivated identity returns deactivated=true, identity not in any tree returns notFound error, malformed base58 DID returns invalidDid error

Write VC issuance tests covering: credential schema validation with ajv, JWT-VC round-trip with did-jwt, JSON-LD proof round-trip with @digitalbazaar/vc, expired credential rejection, tampered credentialSubject rejection

Write interop tests: resolve did:bolyra DID using did-resolver universal resolver shim, verify BolyraHandshakeCredential using vc-js against published context, confirm W3C VC Data Model 2.0 conformance checklist

Draft did:bolyra method specification document following W3C DID Method specification template: abstract, terminology, method name, method-specific identifier, DID Document, operations, security considerations (replay attacks via sessionNonce, Sybil resistance via Semaphore group membership, key compromise recovery), privacy considerations (commitment unlinkability, selective disclosure via ZKP)
Prepare W3C DID Specification Registries submission: fill method name, specification URL, contact, implementation status, and test suite URL fields
dependencies: IdentityRegistry contract (humanTree and agentTree membership storage and query interface)
Semaphore group Merkle tree (BN254 / Poseidon hash — determines identity commitment derivation and key type)
Handshake protocol session result struct (scopeCommitment, sessionNonce fields must be finalized before VC schema locks)
W3C DID Core 1.0 specification
W3C Verifiable Credentials Data Model 2.0 specification
did-resolver npm package (DIDResolver interface contract)
did-jwt or @digitalbazaar/vc (VC signing library — choice affects proof format)
base58btc codec (multiformats or bs58 npm package)
ethers.js v6 (on-chain resolution queries)
JSON-LD context hosting (stable URL required for W3C registry submission)

winner.title: Add explicit less-than constraint for expiry checks in AgentPolicy
winner.description: AgentPolicy performs Num2Bits(64) range checks on expiryTimestamp and currentTimestamp but the circuit source shown is truncated and may lack an explicit LessThan(64) constraint proving currentTimestamp < expiryTimestamp. If this comparison is missing or only checked in Solidity, an attacker can generate a proof with an expired credential and submit it before the contract-side check (or the contract may not re-derive expiry from public signals). Add a LessThan(64) component inside the circuit: isNotExpired.in[0] <== currentTimestamp; isNotExpired.in[1] <== expiryTimestamp; isNotExpired.out === 1. Similarly verify the Delegation circuit enforces delegateeExpiry <= delegatorExpiry inside the SNARK, not just in the contract. Constraint cost is ~130 per LessThan(64).

outline.title: Add explicit less-than constraint for expiry checks in AgentPolicy

artifacts.description: Add LessThan(64) component 'isNotExpired' with currentTimestamp < expiryTimestamp enforced as isNotExpired.out === 1
Add LessThan(64) component enforcing delegateeExpiry <= delegatorExpiry inside the SNARK
Unit tests: valid unexpired credential passes, expired credential fails proof generation, boundary condition currentTimestamp === expiryTimestamp fails
Unit tests: delegatee expiry exceeding delegator expiry fails, equal expiry passes, delegatee expiry strictly less passes
Audit and remove any redundant Solidity-side expiry re-check that was compensating for missing circuit constraint; ensure public signals include both timestamps
Document which expiry/ordering invariants are enforced in-circuit vs on-chain, rationale for in-circuit enforcement, and constraint cost breakdown

steps: Audit AgentPolicy.circom fully: confirm whether LessThan(64) for currentTimestamp < expiryTimestamp is present or absent; document finding
Audit Delegation.circom fully: confirm whether delegateeExpiry <= delegatorExpiry is enforced inside the SNARK or only in Solidity
In AgentPolicy.circom, instantiate component isNotExpired = LessThan(64); wire isNotExpired.in[0] <== currentTimestamp; isNotExpired.in[1] <== expiryTimestamp; add constraint isNotExpired.out === 1

In Delegation.circom, instantiate component delegExpValid = LessThan(64) (or LessEqThan(64)); wire delegExpValid.in[0] <== delegateeExpiry; delegExpValid.in[1] <== delegatorExpiry; add constraint delegExpValid.out === 1
Confirm both currentTimestamp and expiryTimestamp are declared as public signals in AgentPolicy so verifier contracts can observe them if needed
Recompile circuits with circom, regenerate R1CS and witness generation code, verify new constraint counts match expectation (~130 additional constraints per LessThan(64))
Re-run trusted setup or update proving/verification keys for affected circuits
Write failing tests first: craft witness inputs where currentTimestamp >= expiryTimestamp and assert proof generation fails; then confirm fix makes them pass
Write failing tests for Delegation: delegateeExpiry > delegatorExpiry must fail; delegateeExpiry === delegatorExpiry must pass
Audit AgentPolicyVerifier.sol and DelegationVerifier.sol: remove or document any Solidity-side expiry checks that were compensating for the missing circuit constraint
Update specs/expiry-constraint-audit.md with invariant table, constraint costs, and rationale
Run full test suite; confirm no regressions
dependencies: circomlib LessThan(64) or LessEqThan(64) component
circom compiler >= 2.0
snarkjs for R1CS recompilation and key regeneration
Existing AgentPolicy.circom and Delegation.circom source files
Trusted setup ceremony artifacts (must be redone or updated after circuit changes)

winner.title: Add root history buffer for humanTree matching agentTree's 30-root buffer
winner.description: IdentityRegistry maintains a 30-root history buffer for agentTree (agentRootHistory, agentRootExists) but humanTree has no equivalent. The humanMerkleRoot output from HumanUniqueness is verified against a live tree root, meaning any new enrollment between proof generation and on-chain verification invalidates the proof. Add a parallel uint256[30] humanRootHistory with the same rotation logic, update it in enrollHuman(), and check humanMerkleRoot against it in verifyHandshake(). Without this, the protocol has a liveness failure under any realistic enrollment rate.

outline.title: Add root history buffer for humanTree matching agentTree's 30-root buffer

artifacts.description: Add uint256[30] humanRootHistory array and uint256 humanRootHistoryIndex pointer alongside existing agentRootHistory/agentRootIndex. Add humanRootExists mapping for O(1) lookup. Update enrollHuman() to push new humanMerkleRoot into the ring buffer with the same rotation logic used for agentRootHistory. Update verifyHandshake() to check humanMerkleRoot against humanRootExists instead of a live-tree equality check.

Foundry fuzz tests covering: (1) proof accepted when humanMerkleRoot matches any of the 30 historical roots, (2) proof rejected when root is older than buffer window (31st enrollment ago), (3) buffer rotation correctness at index wraparound (index 29 → 0), (4) enrollHuman emits HumanRootAdded event with correct slot, (5) concurrent agent and human enrollments do not corrupt each other's buffers.

Formal specification: invariants (humanRootHistory always contains the last min(n,30) distinct roots; humanRootExists[r] iff r appears in current buffer window), liveness argument (proof generated immediately before any enrollment remains valid for up to 29 subsequent enrollments), security argument (stale root beyond window correctly rejected).
Migration notes: storage layout delta, ABI changes to verifyHandshake, recommended client-side proof freshness window, and upgrade path if IdentityRegistry is behind a proxy.

steps: Audit existing agentRootHistory implementation in IdentityRegistry.sol to extract the exact ring-buffer and mapping pattern to replicate verbatim for humanTree.

Add storage variables: `uint256[30] public humanRootHistory`, `uint256 public humanRootHistoryIndex`, and `mapping(uint256 => bool) public humanRootExists` immediately after their agent counterparts to preserve storage layout coherence.

In enrollHuman(): after computing the new humanMerkleRoot, (a) set humanRootExists[expiredRoot] = false where expiredRoot = humanRootHistory[humanRootHistoryIndex], (b) write humanRootHistory[humanRootHistoryIndex] = newRoot, (c) set humanRootExists[newRoot] = true, (d) increment humanRootHistoryIndex modulo 30. Emit HumanRootAdded(newRoot, humanRootHistoryIndex).
In verifyHandshake(): replace the live-root equality check (`humanMerkleRoot == currentHumanRoot`) with `require(humanRootExists[proof.humanMerkleRoot], 'stale human root')`.
Remove any now-redundant `currentHumanRoot` state variable if it existed only for the equality check; otherwise retain it for off-chain indexing with a clear comment.
Write Foundry unit tests covering the five scenarios listed in the test artifact; ensure all pass with `forge test --match-path test/IdentityRegistry.humanRootBuffer.t.sol -vvv`.
Run `forge coverage` and confirm branch coverage ≥ 95% for the modified functions.
Run slither and aderyn static analysis; resolve any new findings introduced by the storage additions.
Update the formal spec document with invariants and liveness/safety arguments.
Update CHANGELOG and migration docs; if contract is upgradeable, prepare and test the storage-compatible upgrade script.
dependencies: IdentityRegistry.sol — existing agentRootHistory ring-buffer implementation (pattern source)
HumanUniqueness circuit — must output humanMerkleRoot as a public signal consumed by verifyHandshake
enrollHuman() function signature — must remain unchanged externally; only internal state mutations added
Proxy/upgrade pattern (if any) — storage layout must be append-only to avoid slot collisions
Foundry test suite — existing test helpers for tree construction and proof generation

winner.title: Blind scope commitments against bitmask brute-force
winner.description: scopeCommitment in AgentPolicy is Poseidon(permissionBitmask, credentialCommitment). The permissionBitmask is 64 bits with cumulative encoding, yielding at most ~2^16 realistic combinations. An observer who knows the credentialCommitment (public at enrollment) can brute-force the bitmask from the public scopeCommitment, breaking scope privacy. Replace with Poseidon3(permissionBitmask, credentialCommitment, blindingSalt) where blindingSalt is a fresh private input. Update the Delegation circuit's chain-linking check to use the same 3-ary hash. This is a straightforward privacy fix with minimal constraint cost (~800 additional constraints for one extra Poseidon input).

outline.title: Blind scope commitments against bitmask brute-force

artifacts.description: Replace Poseidon(permissionBitmask, credentialCommitment) with Poseidon3(permissionBitmask, credentialCommitment, blindingSalt); add blindingSalt as a private signal input
Update chain-linking check to recompute scopeCommitment using the 3-ary hash; thread blindingSalt as private input through delegation chain verification
Shared component that wraps Poseidon3 for scope commitment; imported by both agent_policy and delegation circuits for consistency
No ABI changes needed (scopeCommitment remains a bytes32 field); update NatSpec comments to document that the commitment now includes a blinding salt
Update commitment builder to generate a cryptographically random 32-byte blindingSalt, compute Poseidon3 off-chain, and return {commitment, blindingSalt} for caller to store securely
Extend AgentPolicyInputs interface with blindingSalt: bigint field; update ProofInputs for delegation to include blindingSalt array per delegation hop
Circom unit tests: verify correct commitment output, verify proof fails with wrong salt, verify brute-force infeasibility by asserting ~2^16 bitmask enumeration cannot match commitment without salt
SDK unit tests: check blindingSalt is fresh on each call, check commitment determinism given same inputs, check round-trip proof generation and verification
Document the privacy threat model (bitmask brute-force attack), the fix rationale, the updated commitment schema, and key management guidance for blindingSalt storage
Migration guide: existing scope commitments must be reissued; enrollment flow changes; backward-incompatible note with upgrade path for live deployments

steps: Add scope_commitment.circom shared component with a Poseidon3(permissionBitmask, credentialCommitment, blindingSalt) template
Update agent_policy.circom: add `signal input blindingSalt` as private input, replace 2-ary Poseidon call with the shared Poseidon3 component
Update delegation.circom: add blindingSalt as private input per hop, thread it through chain-linking recomputation using the same shared component
Recompile circuits, regenerate R1CS and witness generation artifacts, confirm ~800-constraint increase vs baseline
Update SDK scopeCommitment.ts to generate a fresh 32-byte random salt via crypto.getRandomValues, compute Poseidon3 off-chain, and return {commitment, blindingSalt}
Extend SDK types (AgentPolicyInputs, ProofInputs) with the blindingSalt field
Update proof generation helpers to pass blindingSalt into snarkjs/witness generation
Write circuit unit tests covering: correct output, wrong-salt rejection, delegation chain consistency
Write SDK unit tests covering: salt freshness, determinism, proof round-trip
Update specs/scope-commitment-v2.md with threat model, fix rationale, and key management guidance
Write migration guide documenting the breaking change and re-enrollment requirement
Run full test suite; confirm no regressions in existing policy and delegation proof tests
dependencies: circomlib Poseidon3 component (arity-3 variant)
existing agent_policy.circom circuit
existing delegation.circom circuit
snarkjs witness generation pipeline
SDK Poseidon off-chain hasher (circomlibjs or equivalent)
secure randomness source in SDK runtime (crypto.getRandomValues or Node crypto)

winner.title: IETF Internet-Draft: Bolyra Mutual Authentication Protocol
winner.description: Write a complete Internet-Draft (draft-bolyra-mutual-auth-00) with RFC 2119 normative language covering: terminology (identity commitment, credential commitment, scope commitment, nullifier), protocol overview with MSC diagrams for enrollment and handshake flows, wire format using CBOR (RFC 8949) for proof payloads and public signals, circuit specification references, and Security Considerations section addressing the known phantom-delegatee and stale-root issues. This is the foundational document that all other standards work depends on — without it, interop claims are aspirational.

outline.title: IETF Internet-Draft: Bolyra Mutual Authentication Protocol

artifacts.description: Complete IETF Internet-Draft in RFC 7749 (xml2rfc) plain-text output format covering all normative sections
Source xml2rfc v3 markup file for the Internet-Draft, suitable for submission to datatracker.ietf.org
Canonical glossary: identity commitment, credential commitment, scope commitment, nullifier, delegation chain, phantom-delegatee, stale-root — cross-referenced to draft sections
CDDL (RFC 8610) schema for all CBOR-encoded proof payloads, public signals, and protocol messages
ASCII message sequence chart (MSC) for the enrollment flow: Holder ↔ Issuer ↔ Accumulator Service
ASCII MSC for the mutual authentication handshake flow: Prover ↔ Verifier, including nonce exchange and nullifier submission
Normative circuit specification references: identity circuit, credential circuit, scope circuit — input/output signals, constraint counts, and conformance requirements
Standalone security analysis covering phantom-delegatee attack, stale-root vulnerability, nullifier linkability, and replay mitigations — feeds into draft §Security Considerations
CBOR-encoded test vectors for enrollment proof, handshake proof, and negative cases (expired root, replayed nullifier)
IANA registration requests: CBOR tag for proof payload, media type application/bolyra-proof+cbor, and algorithm registry entries

steps: Collect and canonicalize all existing protocol artifacts (circuits, contracts, SDK types) to extract authoritative terminology and signal names
Draft §1 Introduction and §2 Conventions: establish RFC 2119/8174 normative language boilerplate, document scope, and intended use cases (credit-union mutual auth, portable identity)
Draft §3 Terminology: define identity commitment, credential commitment, scope commitment, nullifier, delegation chain, phantom-delegatee, stale-root with precise mathematical notation
Draft §4 Protocol Overview: high-level two-phase description (enrollment, handshake) with informative narrative and references to MSC diagrams
Produce enrollment MSC (msc-enrollment.txt): Holder→Issuer credential issuance, accumulator insertion, commitment broadcast, confirmation receipt
Produce handshake MSC (msc-handshake.txt): Verifier nonce challenge, Prover proof generation, proof submission, nullifier check, accept/reject response
Draft §5 Wire Format: normative CBOR (RFC 8949) encoding for ProofPayload, PublicSignals, EnrollmentRequest, HandshakeRequest, HandshakeResponse — with CDDL schema in wire-format.cddl
Draft §6 Circuit Specification References: normative constraints on identity, credential, and scope circuits — required public inputs, output commitments, and groth16/plonk agility
Draft §7 Security Considerations: phantom-delegatee (delegatee key binding gap), stale-root (accumulator epoch freshness), nullifier replay, proof malleability, side-channel leakage
Draft §8 IANA Considerations: CBOR tag registration, media type registration, algorithm identifiers
Draft §9 References: normative (RFC 2119, RFC 8949, RFC 8610, RFC 7517, Groth16 paper) and informative
Assemble xml2rfc v3 source (draft-bolyra-mutual-auth-00.xml) from all section drafts and validate with xml2rfc toolchain
Generate plain-text output (draft-bolyra-mutual-auth-00.txt) and verify idnits compliance
Produce CBOR conformance test vectors (conformance-vectors.json) covering happy-path enrollment, happy-path handshake, stale-root rejection, and nullifier replay rejection
Cross-check all signal names in circuit-spec.md against actual circuit source files and reconcile any discrepancies
Internal review pass: verify all MUST/SHALL/SHOULD/MAY usage is consistent and each normative claim is backed by a circuit or wire-format constraint
Update terminology.md with final section cross-references and publish as standalone companion document
dependencies: Finalized Circom circuit source files (identity, credential, scope) with confirmed public input/output signal names
Canonical accumulator contract interface (root update cadence, epoch definition)
Nullifier registry interface specification (submission, deduplication semantics)
Agreement on proof system agility scope (Groth16-only v1 vs. PLONK extension point)
CBOR tag range allocation decision (private range vs. IANA early allocation)
